title: Seriphidium terrae-albae
intro: Seriphidium terrae-albae, also known as Wormwood of the White Earth, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a useful remedy for various ailments such as digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Seriphidium terrae-albae include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, and it is also sometimes used in combination with other herbs in traditional medicine practices.
taxonomy: Seriphidium terrae-albae is classified within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. This classification places it within the broader plant kingdom, specifically within the division Magnoliophyta. Further classification includes the subclass Rosidae and the clade flowering plant. The family Asteraceae is commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family.
uses: Seriphidium terrae-albae is a plant species that is not widely studied, and there is limited information available on its medicinal uses.
